title: DHS OIG Launches Eight Concurrent ICE and CBP Oversight Investigations
excerpt: The DHS Office of Inspector General announced eight simultaneous oversight investigations into ICE and CBP operations, including reviews of ICE hiring and training, excessive force accountability, 287(g) supervision, and processes for preventing U.S. citizen arrests.
content:
On February 5, 2026, the DHS Office of Inspector General announced it was conducting eight internal oversight investigations into DHS immigration enforcement operations.

The investigations cover:

  • ICE's hiring and training practices
  • ICE's 287(g) program for collaboration with state and local law enforcement
  • ICE's processes for investigating allegations of excessive force and holding personnel accountable
  • ICE's use of expedited removal authority
  • CBP's interior immigration enforcement
  • DHS's processes for preventing the arrest of U.S. citizens during immigration enforcement operations
  • Unannounced inspections of ICE detention facilities
  • DHS's management of biometrics and personally identifiable information

The announcement did not provide a completion timeline. Members of Congress immediately pushed for an expedited review, noting that the OIG had originally been asked to investigate ICE excessive force in June 2025 but initially deferred. Separately, DHS Secretary Noem requested a list of all ongoing OIG investigations — a move Sen. Tammy Duckworth characterized as an attempt to intimidate the watchdog.
source: Immigration Policy Tracking Project / Congressman Daniel Goldman press release
mediaPlaceholder: OIG announcement document listing eight concurrent DHS immigration enforcement investigations
